metadata.title: Introduction and Interview
metadata.description: Complete IELTS Speaking Part 1 question bank for mock test curriculum. The AI examiner follows the sequence: Introduction -> Opening Frame (R1 or R2) -> Two Topic Frames (from topic_frames pool).

notation_guide.square_brackets: Optional follow-up prompts or examples the examiner may use, e.g. [Why/Why not?]
notation_guide.curly_braces_in_questions: Dynamic placeholders the app fills in at runtime, e.g. {country}
notation_guide.ellipsis_dots: The examiner supplies relevant information from context

step_1_introduction.instruction: Greet the candidate and collect their name. This is NOT timed as part of the 4-5 minutes.
script: Can you tell me your full name, please?
Thank you. And what shall I call you?
And can you tell me where you're from?
Now, in this first part, I'd like to ask you some questions about yourself.

step_2_opening_frame.instruction: Choose ONE R1 variant OR ONE R2 variant at random. Ask all questions in the chosen variant (typically 3).
step_2_opening_frame.for_students: Always use the 'study' path questions from R2. The 'work' path is hidden.
step_2_opening_frame.for_adults: Present the R2 opening question 'Do you work or are you a student?' and branch to the appropriate path.

step_3_topic_frames.instruction: Randomly select TWO topic frames from the topic_frames pool. Ask 3-4 questions from each frame.
step_3_topic_frames.frame_2_questions: 3 or 4 (adjust based on remaining time to stay within 4-5 minutes total)
step_3_topic_frames.intro_phrase: Each frame has an 'intro' field. The examiner says this before asking the questions.

R1_variants.intro: Let's talk about your home town — your home in {country}, or where you live now.
questions: Where is your home town?
Did you learn about the history of your home town when you were at school? [Why/Why not?]
What is your home town famous for today?

R1_variants.intro: Let's talk about where you live now.
questions: Do you live in a town, a city or the countryside? [Why?]
What do you like best about living there? [Why?]
Would you recommend living there to other people? [Why/Why not?]
Is your hometown a city, a town, or a village?
What do you like about your hometown?
Is your hometown suitable for young people? [Why/Why not?]

R1_variants.intro: Let's talk about the house or apartment you're living in now.
questions: How long have you been living in your current home? [Why?]
What do you like most about your home? [Why?]
What kind of home would you like to live in in the future? [Why?]

R1_variants.intro: Let's talk about where you live now.
questions: How long have you lived in your area? [Why?]
What do you like about it? [Why?]
Is there anything you would like to change about where you live? [Why/Why not?]

R2_variants.intro: Let's talk about what you do. Do you work or are you a student?
study_questions: How do you usually spend your day when you're studying? [Why?]
Do you find it easy to organise your study time? [Why/Why not?]
Who helps you most in your studies? [Why?]
work_questions: How do you usually spend your day at work? [Why?]
Do you find it easy to organise your time at work? [Why/Why not?]
Who helps you most in your work? [Why?]

R2_variants.intro: Let's talk about what you do. Do you work or are you a student?
study_questions: What do you enjoy most about your studies? [Why?]
Do you prefer to study at home or to go to a library? [Why?]
Is there something that you would like to study in the future? [Why/Why not?]
work_questions: What do you enjoy most about your work? [Why?]
Would you prefer to work from home or to go out to work? [Why?]
Is there a job that you would like to do in the future? [Why/Why not?]

study_questions: Where is your place of study?
What kind of transport do you use to get to your school? [Why?]
How do you feel when your study day ends? [Why?]
work_questions: What kind of work do you do?
How do you usually get to your workplace? [Why?]
How do you feel at the end of a working day? [Why?]

R2_variants.intro: Let's talk about what you study.
study_questions: Which subjects are you currently studying?
How popular are your subjects in {country}? [Why?]
How important is it for you to study a subject that you enjoy? [Why?]
work_questions: What kind of work do you do?
How popular is your type of work in {country}? [Why?]
How important is it for you to enjoy the work that you do? [Why?]

R2_variants.intro: Let's talk about what you do. Do you work or are you a student?
study_questions: What subject or subjects are you studying?
How do you usually organise your day when you have classes? [Why?]
Do you study better in the morning or later in the day? [Why?]
work_questions: What kind of work do you do?
How do you usually spend a typical workday? [Why?]
Do you prefer working in the morning or the afternoon? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about playing video games.
questions: Have you ever played a video game? [Why/Why not?]
Are you interested in watching other people play video games? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think some people spend too much time playing video games? [Why/Why not?]
Would you prefer to play a video game on your own or with others? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about advertising and adverts.
questions: Do you enjoy watching adverts on TV or the internet? [Why/Why not?]
Is there an advert that you really remember from when you were younger? [What/Why?]
Do you notice advertising in public places, for example on trains or in the street? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to work in advertising? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about remembering to do things.
questions: What do you need to remember to do every day? [Why?]
Is it easy for you to remember to do things? [Why/Why not?]
What do you do to help you remember to do things? [Why?]
Have you ever forgotten to do something important? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about famous people in the news.
questions: Which famous people from {country} are often in the news? [Why?]
Do you enjoy hearing news about famous people? [Why/Why not?]
Do you believe everything you read about famous people? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to be famous and in the news? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about cakes and other sweet things.
questions: Did you enjoy cakes and sweet things when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
When do people in your culture eat special cakes or sweet things? [Why?]
Have you ever made a cake yourself? [Why/Why not?]
How often do you have something sweet at the end of a meal? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about social media, for example Instagram, WhatsApp, or WeChat.
questions: Do most of your friends and family use social media? [Why/Why not?]
When did you start using social media? [Why?]
Do you think you spend too much time using social media? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think people in {country} find social media useful? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about crowded places — that is, places where there are a lot of people.
questions: Are there many crowded places where you live? [Where/Why?]
When was the last time you were in a crowded place? [Why?]
Would you prefer to have a holiday in a crowded place or a quiet place? [Why?]
Do people in {country} often celebrate as part of a crowd, for example at New Year? [Why/Why not?]
How often do you wear sunglasses? [Why/Why not?]
Would you spend a lot of money on sunglasses? [Why/Why not?]
Would you buy sunglasses for someone else as a gift? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever lost any sunglasses? [Why/Why not?]
Do you enjoy singing songs? [Why/Why not?]
Did you learn to sing any songs when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Do people in {country} often sing songs? [Why/Why not?]
Which singers do you enjoy listening to? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about different stages in life.
questions: What did you enjoy most about your childhood?
What's the best thing about being the age you are now? [Why?]
What do you hope you'll be doing in five years' time? [Why?]
How can people remember all the different stages in their lives? [Why/Why not?]
Do you often feel bored? [Why/Why not?]
What sort of things do you find most boring? [Why?]
What do you do to stop yourself feeling bored? [Why?]
Do you get bored more often now than when you were younger? [Why/Why not?]
What colours do you like most? [Why?]
Is colour important to you when you buy clothes? [Why/Why not?]
Do any colours have a special meaning in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
What colour would you choose if you were buying a new car? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about money and paying for things.
questions: Do you generally prefer to spend money or save money? [Why?]
How often do you use a credit card to pay for things? [Why/Why not?]
How do you feel about using a phone app to pay for things? [Why/Why not?]
Will you use cash more often in the future? [Why/Why not?]
Do you enjoy watching sport on TV? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever been to watch a live sports event, for example in a stadium? [Why/Why not?]
Is there a sports event you might like to watch in the future? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think it's more enjoyable to watch sports alone or with other people? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about your daily routine — what you do every day.
questions: What do you usually do in the mornings before starting your work or studies?
Do you prefer to go to bed early or late? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like to do the same things every weekend? [Why/Why not?]
Is your daily routine going to change in the future? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like using a mobile phone? [Why/Why not?]
Do you send many text messages? [Why/Why not?]
When did you first have a mobile phone?
Are there any places where you would not use a mobile phone? [Why?]
Did you learn to draw or paint when you were younger? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever visited an art gallery? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like looking at drawings or paintings? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to learn more about art in the future? [Why/Why not?]
How often do you look at yourself in a mirror? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever bought a mirror? [Why/Why not?]
Would you buy clothes without checking yourself in a mirror first? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think mirrors are a good way to decorate a room? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about shopping at street markets.
questions: What are the best things to buy at street markets in {country}? [Why?]
When was the last time you went to a street market? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like going to street markets when you're on holiday? [Why/Why not?]
Which do you prefer: shopping at a street market or shopping at a shopping centre? [Why?]
How often do you take photos? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like taking photos of yourself? [Why/Why not?]
Do you have a favourite family photo? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to learn how to take better photos? [Why/Why not?]
Is email popular with everyone in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
Do you send a lot of emails? [Why/Why not?]
Do you prefer to email people or speak to them on the phone? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think people will use email more or less in the future? [Why/Why not?]
How often do you remember your dreams after you wake up?
Do you like hearing about other people's dreams? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think dreams can have special meanings? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to learn more about the subject of dreams? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about finding and losing things.
questions: What kinds of lost things have you found in a public place?
What would you do if you found something valuable, for example a phone? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever lost something which was found by a stranger? [What was it/Why?]
Would you ever ask for help on social media to find something you'd lost? [Why/Why not?]
Did you enjoy travelling by car when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Would you generally prefer to be a driver or a passenger in a car? [Why/Why not?]
How important is the colour of a car to you? [Why/Why not?]
What type of car would you most like to have in the future? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about going to the cinema.
questions: Did you go to the cinema a lot when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Do you still like the same films that you liked when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Do you prefer to go to the cinema with friends or on your own? [Why/Why not?]
Which is more popular in {country}: watching films at the cinema, or watching films at home? [Why/Why not?]
What kinds of websites do you use most often? [Why?]
Do you prefer to get information from websites or books? [Why/Why not?]
Does your favourite website look the same now as when you first used it? [Why/Why not?]
Which is more important to you: what a website looks like, or how easy it is to use? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about feeling calm and relaxed.
questions: Have you visited a place recently where you felt calm and relaxed? [Where/Why?]
Do you find listening to music a relaxing thing to do? [Why/Why not?]
Would you ever do exercise to help you feel calm and relaxed? [Why/Why not?]
Are you usually a calm and relaxed person? [Why?]
Do you have a favourite teacher at school? [Why/Why not?]
How have teachers helped you? [Why?]
Are you still in contact with any of your teachers from primary school? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to be a teacher? [Why/Why not?]
Did you ride a bicycle when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Do you have a bicycle now? [Why/Why not?]
Are bicycles popular in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think bicycles will be more or less popular in the future? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about meeting new people.
questions: Do you like meeting new people? [Why/Why not?]
Have you met anyone new recently? [Who/Why not?]
Did you meet new people more often when you were younger? [Why/Why not?]
What do you usually talk about when you meet someone for the first time? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about coins — money made of metal, like cents or pennies.
questions: Do you like to have some coins with you when you go out? [Why/Why not?]
Did you use coins more often in the past? [Why/Why not?]
When you were a child, did you ever save coins in a money box? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think that collecting unusual coins is an interesting hobby to have? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.topic: Laughing and Things That Are Funny
topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about laughing and things that are funny.
questions: Do you enjoy watching films that are funny? [Why/Why not?]
When was the last time something made you laugh? [What was it/Why?]
Are you good at making people laugh? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think it's always good to make people laugh? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about roads and streets.
questions: Are the roads and streets very busy near where you live? [Why/Why not?]
How easy is it to cross the roads in your area? [Why?]
Is there anything you would like to change about the roads in your area? [What/Why/Why not?]
Is there a road or street in {country} that is very famous? [What/Why?]
Where did you like to play when you were a child? [Why?]
Who did you spend most time with when you were a child? [Why?]
Is there anything you enjoyed doing as a child that you still enjoy now? [What/Why/Why not?]
What do you think is the best thing about being a child? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about views people enjoy looking at.
questions: Are there any places with a good view where you live? [Why/Why not?]
What's the best view you've ever seen? [Why?]
Do you always take photos when you see a good view? [Why/Why not?]
Do you try to have a room with a good view when you're on holiday? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like to wear perfume or aftershave? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever bought a bottle of perfume as a present? [Why/Why not?]
Do you have a favourite type of perfume? [What/Why/Why not?]
Would you pay a lot for a bottle of perfume? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about fish and fishing.
questions: Did you ever go fishing when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
How popular is fishing as a hobby in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
Do you like eating fish? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever been to a fish market? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about working or studying in groups.
questions: Did you work in groups when you were a child at primary school? [Why/Why not?]
Do you often work or study in a group now? [Why/Why not?]
How have you benefited from working in groups? [Why?]
Is there anything you don't like about working in groups? [Why?]
How often do you wear a T-shirt? [Why/Why not?]
Did you wear T-shirts when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Would you ever give someone a T-shirt as a present? [Who/Why/Why not?]
Do you often look at the T-shirts that other people are wearing? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about tea and coffee.
questions: Is drinking tea and coffee common in your area? [Why/Why not?]
Do you ever go out just for tea or coffee? [Why/Why not?]
When was the last time you had tea or coffee? [Why?]
Would you serve tea or coffee to a guest? [Why/Why not?]
Can you play any musical instruments? [Why/Why not?]
Do you find learning an instrument easy? [Why/Why not?]
Did you study music much at school? [Why/Why not?]
Should every child get music education at school? [Why/Why not?]
How interested are you in robots? [Why/Why not?]
As a child, did you like films with robots in them? [Why/Why not?]
Would you want a robot assistant at home? [Why/Why not?]
Would you be comfortable in a robot-driven vehicle? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about chatting with friends.
questions: Do you chat with friends regularly? [Why/Why not?]
Which kind of chatting do you like more: in person or on social media? [Why/Why not?]
What's more fun for you: group chats or one-on-one chats? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever disagreed with a friend during a chat? [Why/Why not?]
What do you prefer: comfortable clothes or formal clothes? [Why?]
Do you take much time picking out outfits each morning? [Why/Why not?]
As a child, did you get to choose your own clothes? [Why/Why not?]
What do you do with clothes you don't wear anymore? [Why?]
Were you into running as a child? [Why/Why not?]
When did you last go running? [Why?]
What's your opinion on running for fitness? [Why?]
Are there any good places to run in your area? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about films and movies.
questions: What kind of films do you like most? [Why?]
Is watching movies a good activity to do with friends? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever thought of making a video yourself? [Why/Why not?]
Do you prefer watching films at home or at the cinema? [Why/Why not?]
Are there any loud noises around your home? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like a quieter living environment? [Why/Why not?]
Do you enjoy playing loud music? [Why/Why not?]
Is it acceptable for people to be noisy in public areas? [Why/Why not?]
How often do you use maps on your mobile phone? [Why/Why not?]
Would you ever consider using a paper map? [Why/Why not?]
Is it easy for you to read a map? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever thought of hanging a map on a wall at home? [Why/Why not?]
Did you like geography at school? [Why/Why not?]
Is your geography knowledge useful today? [Why/Why not?]
Are you interested in the geography of foreign countries? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever thought of being a geography teacher? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about presents and gifts.
questions: Have you ever received an amazing gift? [What was it?]
Are you good at selecting gifts for others? [Why/Why not?]
What kind of gift would you give a friend? [Why?]
Have you ever given a handmade present? [Why/Why not?]
In what ways do you help others? [Why?]
Did your parents encourage you to be helpful? [Why/Why not?]
Do you enjoy helping others? [Why/Why not?]
When did someone last help you out? [How?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about ambitions and dreams.
questions: What job did you want to do when you were a child? [Why?]
What job do you want in the future? [Why?]
Are you a person with big goals? [Why/Why not?]
Do you have any personal dreams you'd like to achieve? [Why?]
Do you try to fix things that are broken? [Why/Why not?]
Did someone teach you how to fix things? [Who/Why?]
What do you do with things you can't fix? [Why?]
Should everyone learn to fix things? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about how you spend your week.
questions: Which day of the week do you like the most? [Why?]
Do you do the same things on the same days every week? [Why/Why not?]
Which day is the busiest for you? [Why?]
Is there anything you would like to change about your weekly routine? [Why/Why not?]
Do you eat ice cream a lot? [Why/Why not?]
Did you eat more ice cream when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Is there a good place to buy ice cream where you live? [Why?]
Would you like to learn how to make ice cream yourself? [Why/Why not?]
Does it rain a lot where you live? [Why/Why not?]
Would you prefer to live somewhere wet or somewhere dry? [Why?]
Do you like going out when it's raining? [Why/Why not?]
Has rain ever changed your plans? [Why/Why not?]
Are there many wild animals in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
Have you seen any programmes about wild animals on TV? [Which ones?]
Would you go to a zoo? [Why/Why not?]
Which wild animal would you most like to see in nature? [Why?]
How do you try to stay healthy?
What kind of exercise do you enjoy? [Why?]
Did you learn about staying healthy at school? [How?]
Is it easy for people in your area to stay healthy? [Why/Why not?]
Did you have a bag for school when you were young? [Why/Why not?]
Do you carry a bag with you now? [Why/Why not?]
What kind of bag do you use when you travel? [Why?]
Would you like to receive a bag as a gift? [Why/Why not?]
What languages did you study at school? [Why?]
Did you enjoy language classes? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think people should learn other languages? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to learn a new language in the future? [Why/Why not?]
What do you like to do with your friends? [Why?]
Is it easy for you to see your friends regularly? [Why/Why not?]
Have you had some friends for a long time? [Why/Why not?]
How important are friends to you? [Why?]
Did you find it easy to pay attention at school? [Why/Why not?]
Can you focus well in noisy places? [Why/Why not?]
What do you do to help yourself concentrate better?
Would you like a job where you need to concentrate a lot? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about having a day off.
questions: When was your last day off? [What did you do?]
What do you usually do on your days off? [Why?]
Do you prefer to spend your days off with friends or family? [Why?]
What would you do if you had tomorrow off? [Why?]
Did you enjoy science classes when you were at school? [Why/Why not?]
Would you like to learn more about science? [Why/Why not?]
Do you watch science-related programmes on TV? [Why/Why not?]
Are science museums popular in {country}? [Why/Why not?]
Did you wear a watch when you were a child? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever been given a watch as a gift? [Who gave it to you?]
Do you wear a watch now? [Why/Why not?]
Why do you think some people prefer expensive watches?

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about sport and physical activities.
questions: Did you learn to play any sports when you were a child? [Which ones?]
Do you think all children should play sports at school? [Why/Why not?]
What sports do you enjoy playing or watching? [Why?]
Are there any sports you would like to try in the future? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about computers and tablets.
questions: What do you mostly use a computer or tablet for? [Why?]
Do you remember when you first used a computer or tablet? [When was it?]
Do you ever find computers or tablets difficult to use? [Why/Why not?]
How would your life be different if computers didn't exist?

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about skills and talents.
questions: What are some things you think you are good at? [Why?]
Do you have a talent that someone else in your family also has? [Why/Why not?]
Is there a skill or talent you would like to improve? [Why?]
Do you think your talents will help you in your future career? [Why/Why not?]
Have you ever visited an important old building? [Why/Why not?]
Is there an old building you would like to visit one day? [Why/Why not?]
Do you think it's important to protect old buildings? [Why/Why not?]
Would you rather live in an old building or a modern one? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about books and reading.
questions: Do you read more or less now than when you were younger? [Why?]
Have you ever watched a film and then read the book it was based on? [Why/Why not?]
When do you usually find time to read? [Why?]
How often do you buy books? [Why?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about what you do in the evenings.
questions: What do you usually do in the evening? [Why?]
Do you spend your evenings differently on weekdays compared to weekends? [Why/Why not?]
How did you spend your evenings when you were a child? [Why?]
Is there anything you dislike doing in the evening? [Why/Why not?]

topic_frames.intro: Let's talk about things you keep.
questions: Do you still have many things from your childhood? [Why/Why not?]
Do you prefer keeping things for a long time, or throwing them away? [Why?]
Where do you usually keep things you use every day, like keys or money? [Why?]
Do you like to keep all the photos you take? [Why/Why not?]
Do you often have to sit for long periods? [Why/Why not?]
When you were a child, did you enjoy sitting on the floor? [Why/Why not?]
Can you fall asleep easily while sitting in a chair? [Why/Why not?]
Do you have a favourite place to sit? [Why/Why not?]
